NetScript
  • Tutorials
  • How-to guides
  • Reference
  • Explanation

Explanation

Explanation is understanding-oriented: discussion that illuminates how NetScript works and why it is designed the way it is — architecture, plugin model, and trade-offs. It is for reading and reflection rather than step-by-step doing. To get started practically use the tutorials, to solve a concrete task see the how-to guides, and to look up exact APIs use the reference.

Pages

  • The NetScript architecture — the published-surface thesis, contracts-first model, the six package archetypes, and how plugins extend the framework.
  • The plugin model — how plugins relate to their core packages, manifests and contributions, and how the host discovers them through generated registries.

NetScript user documentation.
